frameworks.name: attentional priority
frameworks.one_line: temporal-order recency confers higher priority that modulates working memory representations

frameworks.name: inverted encoding model
frameworks.one_line: population-level reconstruction of orientation channels from fMRI activity

frameworks.name: frontoparietal control of visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: debate over whether parietal and frontal cortex carry mnemonic content

core_question: Do mnemonic representations in parietal and frontal cortex depend on temporal-order-based attentional priority?
key_assumptions: the most recent item carries higher attentional priority
orientation-selective channels can be recovered by an inverted encoding model
early visual cortex provides a baseline of order-invariant maintenance
manipulations: serial order of the cued grating (first vs second of two)
region of interest (V1-V3 vs IPS vs FEF)
key_findings: early visual cortex (V1-V3) reconstructed orientations robustly regardless of serial order
IPS and FEF showed stronger orientation representations when the second (more recent) grating was remembered
interpretations: mnemonic codes in frontoparietal cortex are gated by temporal-order-based attentional priority
variability in prior frontoparietal WM findings may reflect unmodeled attentional-priority signals
